meta.feed_title: Latest World News on Fox News

items.title: Inside Tehran after strikes: Iranian woman describes fear, checkpoints and people used as ‘human shields’
items.summary: An anonymous Iranian woman detailed to The Australian the nightly explosions, celebrations and fear in Tehran as Trump's two-week ceasefire with Iran tentatively took hold.

items.title: Israeli man built bomb lab for Iranian plot targeting ex-PM Bennett, authorities say
items.summary: A Haifa man was allegedly recruited by an Iranian handler to build bombs for an assassination plot believed to target ex-PM Naftali Bennett.

items.title: UK defense minister warns Putin of 'serious consequences' after covert underwater military operation
items.summary: U.K. Defense Minister John Healey has revealed a major military operation involving the Royal Navy and Norway to deter Russian submarines in the North Sea.

items.title: Strait of Hormuz toll would set 'dangerous precedent,' UN shipping agency warns
items.summary: The U.N.'s shipping agency warned on Thursday that any toll imposed on ships passing through the Strait of Hormuz would set a "dangerous precedent."

items.title: Miner rescued alive after spending nearly 2 weeks trapped underground in flooded area
items.summary: A Mexican miner was rescued after spending 13 days trapped underground, officials said, while one miner was found dead and another remains missing.

items.title: US allowing some Nigeria embassy staff to evacuate over 'deteriorating security situation'
items.summary: State Department authorized departure of some U.S. embassy staff in Nigeria over a "deteriorating security situation" linked to terrorism and violence.

items.title: Chief prosecutor behind Israel war crimes charges faces disciplinary action amid sexual misconduct allegations
items.summary: ICC Chief Prosecutor Karim Khan faces disciplinary proceedings over alleged sexual misconduct after the Bureau of the Assembly of States Parties voted to move forward with action.

items.title: Iran threatens to end ceasefire over Hezbollah's exclusion from truce deal
items.summary: Iran says the U.S. must choose between ceasefire or war via Israel as Tuesday's ceasefire deal excludes Tehran-backed terrorist group Hezbollah.

items.title: UK's NATO show of force ends with docked destroyer in Mediterranean after 'technical' issue
items.summary: The U.K. docked its sole naval warship in the Mediterranean due to technical issues amid growing criticism of PM Starmer's response to the Iran war.

items.title: Evangelical leaders rally for Trump and Israel as Operation Epic Fury reshapes the region
items.summary: Evangelical leaders say support for Israel has remained strong during Operation Epic Fury, the joint U.S.-Israel military campaign against Iran, as a new ceasefire takes hold.

items.title: North Korea fires missiles toward sea after ridiculing South's hopes for better ties
items.summary: North Korea fired multiple ballistic missiles after criticizing South Korea’s push for improved relations, raising tensions in the region.

items.title: Iran's UN ambassador takes swipe at Trump in final hours before Strait of Hormuz deadline
items.summary: Iran's UN ambassador is calling one of President Donald Trump's posts on Truth Social on Tuesday “deeply irresponsible" and “profoundly alarming."

items.title: Australia’s most decorated living soldier charged amid fierce debate over war crimes allegations
items.summary: Australia's most decorated living soldier Ben Roberts-Smith arrested and charged with allegedly murdering five unarmed Afghans in Afghanistan.

items.title: China aiding Iran missile program amid US-Israeli strikes, reports say
items.summary: China is reportedly sending missile fuel materials to Iran to help rebuild its missile program amid continuing U.S.-Israeli strikes aimed against the Tehran regime.

items.title: Russia, China veto UN resolution aimed at reopening Strait of Hormuz, hours before Trump deadline
items.summary: Russia and China vetoed a U.N. resolution to reopen the Strait of Hormuz hours before President Donald Trump's deadline for Iran to stop threatening the vital waterway.

items.title: US embassies in Bahrain, Egypt issue warnings as Iran threatens universities across Middle East
items.summary: The U.S. Embassy in Bahrain orders Americans to shelter in place, warning that Iran may target American universities in the country and across the Middle East.

items.title: Iran releases 2 French citizens after 'three and a half years of detention,' Macron says
items.summary: French nationals Cécile Kohler and Jacques Paris were released by Iran after three and a half years of detention, President Macron announced Tuesday.

items.title: 3 gunmen open fire outside Israeli consulate in Istanbul, dubbed 'terrorists' by Turkish official
items.summary: One attacker is dead and two others injured after a gunfight with police outside the Israeli consulate in Istanbul, Turkey's interior minister says.

items.title: Mauro compares Iran rescue of missing colonel to Maduro capture, credits intelligence preparation
items.summary: Paul Mauro says U.S. intelligence agencies had already laid the groundwork needed to locate and rescue a missing colonel from enemy territory in Iran.

items.title: CENTCOM commander directed strike against an IRGC headquarters in underground facility: sources
items.summary: U.S. Central Command Commander Admiral Brad Cooper directed a strike on an IRGC headquarters in an underground facility, sources indicated.

items.title: Iran executes people, including teens, by hanging
items.summary: The nation of Iran has been carrying out executions, including of teenagers, as the U.S. and Israel wage war against the country, reports indicate.

items.title: 'Credible intelligence' reveals North Korea's successor to Kim Jong Un, South Korea says
items.summary: South Korea's National Intelligence Service thinks North Korean figure Kim Jong Un's daughter has been lined up to become Kim's eventual successor, lawmakers noted, according to Reuters.

items.title: IDF confirms IRGC intel chief killed; Quds Force commander also eliminated in strike
items.summary: Israel announces IRGC intelligence chief Majid Khademi was killed in a precision strike that also took out Quds Force special operations commander Bagheri.

items.title: Baby among 3 dead in holiday horror as Easter egg hunt turns deadly
items.summary: Three people were killed after high winds knocked down a tree onto a group of people in Germany during an Easter egg hunt on Sunday morning, police said.

items.title: Inside Iran’s ruling ideology: How a ‘holy mission’ and messianic doctrine fuel regime extremism
items.summary: Iranian journalist says Iran's regime uses messianic Mahdi doctrine to claim divine authority and make political compromise nearly impossible.
